title: Nested Tool Calls
constraints: The agent must call find_user first to get the user ID
The user ID from find_user must be passed to get_orders
The agent must not hallucinate or hardcode user IDs
No changes to the tool implementations
hints: Update the system prompt to explicitly instruct the agent to first look up the user, then use the returned ID for subsequent calls
Make sure the agent extracts just the ID from the first tool's output rather than passing the entire response string
In LangGraph, you can model this as two sequential nodes where the first node's output feeds into the second

evaluationCriteria.label: find_user is called first
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent always calls find_user to get the user ID before calling get_orders

evaluationCriteria.label: Correct ID is passed to get_orders
evaluationCriteria.description: The user ID returned by find_user is extracted and passed to get_orders

evaluationCriteria.label: No hallucinated IDs
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent never invents or guesses user IDs — it always looks them up first
